claim.content:
Define $v_0=10^{6}\ \mathrm{m}\,\mathrm{s}^{-1}$ (the monolayer graphene band velocity), $\kappa=4\pi/(3L_{\mathrm{m}})$ (the mini-Brillouin-zone corner momentum), $w_1$ an interlayer tunnelling energy, and $\alpha=w_1/(v_0\kappa)$.  The proposition states that the twist-angle dependence of the squared effective Dirac-like velocity can be captured approximately by
      $\dfrac{v_{\mathrm{F}}^2}{v_0^2}=\Big(\dfrac{1-3\alpha^2}{1+6\alpha^2}\Big)^2+\delta v_{\mathrm{F}}^2,$
      where $\delta v_{\mathrm{F}}$ is a phenomenological positive constant introduced to avoid $v_{\mathrm{F}}=0$ at the magic angle within this simple approximation.  The weak point is that this expression imports a continuum-model expansion augmented by an ad hoc broadening term $\delta v_{\mathrm{F}}$, and it may not quantitatively capture the true twist-angle dependence of the low-energy velocity in interacting, reconstructed bands of real magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene.
      Bistritzer, R. & MacDonald, A. H. Moiré bands in twisted double-layer graphene. Proc. Natl Acad. Sci. USA 108, 12233–12237 (2011).
